steps.title: Estimate intercept and slope
steps.content: The scatterplot starts near $y=90$ when $x=0$ and falls to near $y=0$ when $x$ is about $9$. That is a drop of about $90$ over a run of about $9$, so the slope is about $-10$ and the intercept is about $90$.

steps.title: Graph the choices
steps.content: Use Desmos as an answer-choice overlay: graph all four equations and compare them with the plotted trend. Choices B and C are too flat because their slope is only $-0.1$. Choice A has the right slope but starts at $80$, too low for the graph.

steps.title: Check a midpoint
steps.content: At $x=4$, the plotted trend is near $y=50$. Choice D gives $90-10(4)=50$, while choice A gives $80-10(4)=40$. The correct model is $y=90-10x$, so choice D is correct.
